edges.description: Anterior-posterior patterning disruption converts the thumb toward a finger-like triphalangeal identity.
Ectopic anterior SHH signaling produces supernumerary digits on the radial side of the hand.
The disrupted limb-patterning program produces the polysyndactyly component of the TPT-PS phenotype.

edges.target: ZRS regulatory variants causing ectopic SHH expression
ZRS regulatory variants causing ectopic SHH expression

nodes.description: Ectopic anterior SHH creates a mirror-image morphogen gradient in the limb bud, leading to duplication of posterior digit identities on the anterior (radial) side. This produces supernumerary preaxial digits and conversion of the two-phalanx thumb to a three-phalanx finger-like digit.

evidence.reference_title: A microduplication of the long range SHH limb regulator (ZRS) is associated with triphalangeal thumb-polysyndactyly syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Point mutations within this non-coding regulatory region designated ZRS lead to ectopic expression of Shh in the anterior margin of the limb bud, as shown in mice, and cause the human triphalangeal thumb and polysyndactyly (TPT-PS) phenotype.

evidence.reference_title: A novel ZRS mutation leads to preaxial polydactyly type 2 in a heterozygous form and Werner mesomelic syndrome in a homozygous form.
evidence.snippet: A mouse transgenic enhancer assay shows that this mutation causes an expansion of the enhancer's expression domain in the developing mouse limb, confirming its pathogenicity.

nodes.description: Point mutations or microduplications in the ZRS enhancer cause gain-of-function ectopic expression of SHH in the anterior limb bud mesenchyme. Normally SHH is restricted to the zone of polarizing activity (ZPA) in the posterior limb bud; ectopic anterior expression disrupts the morphogen gradient that specifies digit identity along the anterior-posterior axis.

evidence.reference_title: A variant in the sonic hedgehog regulatory sequence (ZRS) is associated with triphalangeal thumb and deregulates expression in the developing limb.
evidence.snippet: we utilized a mutated ZRS construct to demonstrate that it can drive ectopic anterior expression of a reporter gene in the developing mouse forelimb.

evidence.reference_title: Preaxial polydactyly/triphalangeal thumb is associated with changed transcription factor-binding affinity in a family with a novel point mutation in the long-range cis-regulatory element ZRS.
evidence.snippet: Electrophoretical mobility shift assay demonstrated a marked difference between wild-type and the mutant probe, which uniquely bound one or several transcription factors extracted from Caco-2 cells.

nodes.description: Extra digits on the radial (thumb) side of the hand, reflecting ectopic anterior SHH signaling in the limb bud.

evidence.reference_title: A novel ZRS mutation leads to preaxial polydactyly type 2 in a heterozygous form and Werner mesomelic syndrome in a homozygous form.
evidence.snippet: Although most mutations cause preaxial polydactyly (PPD), triphalangeal thumb (TPT) or both, a mutation in position 404 of the ZRS causes more severe Werner mesomelic syndrome (WMS)

evidence.reference_title: Preaxial polydactyly/triphalangeal thumb is associated with changed transcription factor-binding affinity in a family with a novel point mutation in the long-range cis-regulatory element ZRS.
evidence.snippet: This finding supports a model in which ectopic anterior SHH expression in the developing limb results from abnormal binding of one or more transcription factors to the mutant sequence.

nodes.description: Fusion of digits, variably present in TPT-PS families.

evidence.reference_title: The pZRS non-coding regulatory mutation resulting in triphalangeal thumb-polysyndactyly syndrome changes the pattern of local interactions.
evidence.snippet: we report on a large Polish family presenting with a classical triphalangeal thumb-polysyndactyly syndrome (TPT-PS).

nodes.description: Thumb contains three phalanges instead of the normal two, resembling a finger. May be unilateral or bilateral.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel point mutations in the long-range SHH enhancer in three families with triphalangeal thumb and preaxial polydactyly.
evidence.snippet: Four North American Caucasian families were identified with autosomal dominant triphalangeal thumb.

evidence.reference_title: A variant in the sonic hedgehog regulatory sequence (ZRS) is associated with triphalangeal thumb and deregulates expression in the developing limb.
evidence.snippet: We found this variant in three independently ascertained probands from southern England with triphalangeal thumb, demonstrated significant linkage of the phenotype to the variant (LOD = 4.1)

nodes.description: Multiple point mutations within the 774-bp conserved ZRS element alter transcription factor binding sites, causing ectopic anterior SHH expression in the developing limb.

evidence.reference_title: Two novel point mutations in the long-range SHH enhancer in three families with triphalangeal thumb and preaxial polydactyly.
evidence.snippet: Mutations within ZRS appear to be a common cause of familial triphalangeal thumb and preaxial polydactyly.

nodes.id: ZRS regulatory variants causing ectopic SHH expression

evidence.reference_title: Two novel point mutations in the long-range SHH enhancer in three families with triphalangeal thumb and preaxial polydactyly.
evidence.snippet: Spatio-temporal expression of sonic hedgehog (SHH) is driven by a regulatory element (ZRS) that lies 1 Mb upstream from SHH.

evidence.reference_title: A variant in the sonic hedgehog regulatory sequence (ZRS) is associated with triphalangeal thumb and deregulates expression in the developing limb.
evidence.snippet: A locus for triphalangeal thumb, variably associated with pre-axial polydactyly, was previously identified in the zone of polarizing activity regulatory sequence (ZRS), a long range limb-specific enhancer of the Sonic Hedgehog (SHH) gene at human chromosome 7q36.3.

evidence.reference_title: The pZRS non-coding regulatory mutation resulting in triphalangeal thumb-polysyndactyly syndrome changes the pattern of local interactions.
evidence.snippet: This rare congenital limb anomaly is generally caused by microduplications encompassing the Sonic Hedgehog (SHH) limb enhancer, termed the zone of polarizing activity (ZPA) regulatory sequence (ZRS).

nodes.description: A pathogenic variant in the pre-ZRS (pZRS), a conserved sequence near the ZRS, causes increased interactions within the SHH regulatory domain (SHH-LMBR1 TAD).

evidence.reference_title: The pZRS non-coding regulatory mutation resulting in triphalangeal thumb-polysyndactyly syndrome changes the pattern of local interactions.
evidence.snippet: The results of 4C-seq experiments revealed increased interactions within the whole SHH regulatory domain (SHH-LMBR1 TAD) in the patient compared to controls.
